name: Oromia Region (Ethiopia)

colors.symbolism: the youth and future of the Oromo people
the elders, and the blood shed in struggles for freedom
the central tree — fertility and the Oromo gadaa system

description: Three horizontal stripes of black (top), white (middle), and red (bottom), with a stylised odaa tree (sycomore fig) in green at the centre of the white stripe.
history: The Oromia Region is the largest regional state of the Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia, home to the Oromo people. The flag was officially adopted with the establishment of the regional state under the 1995 Constitution. The odaa tree is sacred in the traditional Oromo gadaa system, under which assemblies were held in its shade.

features.symbolism: the sacred tree under which the Oromo gadaa assemblies were held
